zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Beauchamp於1249年之前出生在英國薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘時，他的父親Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch11245歲，母親Alice de Mohun26歲。他於大約1270年與Cecily de Vivonne在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他去世於1283年10月24日在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國薩默塞特斯托克 素布 哈姆頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When John de Beauchamp was born before 1249, in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, his father, Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch, was 11245 and his mother, Alice de Mohun, was 26. He married Cecily de Vivonne about 1270, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 8 daughters. He died on 24 October 1283, in his hometown, and was buried in Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de Beauchamp est né avant 1249, à Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch, avait 11245 ans et sa mère, Alice de Mohun, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Cecily de Vivonne environ 1270, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 8 filles. Il est décédé le 24 octobre 1283, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, Angleterre.

de.Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, Unit: Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als John de Beauchamp vor 1249 in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch 11245 und seine Mutter Alice de Mohun 26. Er hat um 1270 in England, United Kingdom Cecily de Vivonne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 8 Töchter. Er starb am 24. Oktober 1283 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John de Beauchamp è nato il prima del 1249 nel Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, il suo padre, Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch, aveva 11245 anni e il suo madre, Alice de Mohun, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Cecily de Vivonne circa 1270, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 8 figlie. Morì il 24 ottobre 1283, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John de Beauchampが1249年以前にイギリスソメルセトハトチ ビーウチャンップで生まれた時，父Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatchは11245歳，母Alice de Mohunは26歳であった。約1270年にイギリスイギリスでCecily de Vivonneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼は1283年10月24日に故郷で死去し，イギリスソメルセトストク スブ ハムドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John de Beauchamp(이)가 1249년 이전 영국 서머세트 해트치 보챔프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch(은)는 11245세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Mohun(은)는 26세였다. 그는 대략 1270년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Cecily de Vivonne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1283년 10월 24일 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 스토크 수브 함던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John de Beauchamp nasceu antes 1249, em Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch, tinha 11245 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Mohun, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Cecily de Vivonne aproximadamente 1270, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 8 filhas. Ele faleceu em 24 de outubro de 1283, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de Beauchamp родился перед 1249 г. в местности хач бочамп, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch было 11245 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Mohun, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Cecily de Vivonne около 1270 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 8 дочерей. Он умер 24 октября 1283 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности сток суб хамдон, сомирсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John de Beauchamp nació antes de 1249, en Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch, tenía 11245 años y su mamá, Alice de Mohun, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Cecily de Vivonne aproximadamente 1270, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió el 24 de octubre de 1283, en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, Inglaterra.
